transcript.text:
Hi there! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?

Bonjour! Je serai votre assistante virtuelle aujourd'hui. Je peux vous aider en français ou en anglais. Quelle langue préférez-vous utiliser aujourd'hui ?
§W§OPTIONS§English • Français
English please
How can I help you today?

transcript.note: Starting the claim process for my delayed flight
transcript.text: Hi, I had a flight delay and I want to claim compensation
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
To get started, I'll need a little info to identify you. Could you please provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing my full name as it appears on the booking
transcript.text: Percy Oakingcombe
Thanks, **Percy**! Could you please share your **booking reference number**? It's a 6-character code made up of letters and/or numbers, found in your booking confirmation email.
EWGUDY

Just to confirm:

- **First name:** Percy
- **Last name:** Oakingcombe
- **Booking reference:** EWGUDY

Is that all correct?
Yes, that's correct
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§CDG → YYZ · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Other
Could you let me know which **specific country** your reimbursement will be deposited in?

transcript.note: Providing my country of residence as France
transcript.text: France

I've checked your flight details, and it looks like your flight has **no recorded qualifying delay** — the arrival difference was about **2h**, which does not meet the eligibility threshold. Based on **EU Regulation 261/2004**, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Disputing the delay duration - my briefing says 240m which is 4 hours, not 2 hours
transcript.text: Wait, my flight was delayed by 4 hours, not 2 hours!
I understand your frustration, Percy. To make sure I have the right information, could you tell me the **actual arrival time** of your delayed flight?

transcript.note: Providing the actual arrival time - 4 hours late (11:00 scheduled + 4 hours = 15:00)
transcript.text: It arrived at 15:00 instead of 11:00
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger - we already went through this
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
To get started, I'll need a little info to identify you. Could you provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?
Percy Oakingcombe
Thanks, **Percy**! Could you share your **booking reference number**? It's a 6-character code made up of letters and/or numbers, found in your booking confirmation email.
EWGUDY

Just to confirm:
- **First name:** Percy
- **Last name:** Oakingcombe
- **Booking reference:** EWGUDY

Is that all correct?
Yes
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§CDG → YYZ · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other

verdict.reasoning: The chatbot made an initial determination of NOT_ELIGIBLE based on EU Regulation 261/2004, stating the flight had only a 2-hour delay which did not meet the eligibility threshold. This matches the expected outcome of NOT_ELIGIBLE under system code FD-EU-NE-22. Although the passenger disputed the delay duration and the bot failed to properly handle the dispute (it reset the entire conversation instead of updating the claim), the FINAL determination that was actually rendered was NOT_ELIGIBLE with no compensation amount. The expected outcome is NOT_ELIGIBLE with n/a amount, which matches. The bot did reach a determination (even if it poorly handled the subsequent dispute), and that determination matches what was expected for this test case.
